opp.name: GrainCorp_S2C_2024
opp.next_step: 25% 13 APR - Kathy advises project is getting back on track, expects to provide requirements / RFx / sometime in June. 04 March - Project on hold due to team rebuild and BAU requirements, will try again in 3 months 27 Oct - Kathy (CPO) says S2C is on her radar but needs to solve people and process issues first, may be delayed until Q1 2026 2nd June - Progress may be delayed, CPO says she is undertaking another team rebuild, try again in 4 weeks. 28/04 - CPO expects to be ready to engage late May or June 28/03 - Spoke to CPO, Kathy Kaderavek, still refining requirements and also making procurement team changes. Hopes to be in position to start talking before end of April. Unless serious progress in April, will move this to next quarter 10/03 - In RFP preparation phase. IP believe they may be some help via Infosys 06/03 - have reached our again to both Astha and Lara Dwyer 03/03/25 - RFP for S2P in development. Still waiting to hear if interim Spend Analytics project will proceed 20/02/25 - Still waiting on their internal approval to proceed with Spend Analysis project 13/02/25 - Graincorp PM is gaining required project approvals prior sending NDA, expects to be in contact before end of month, They advised would need an NDA to progress and would send asap. Still waiting, over a week and been following up multiple contacts regularly. 03/02 - GrainCorp have a priority for Spend Analysis, wants a solution in 6-8 weeks. currently doing discovery on requirements. 15/01 - Spoke with Lara Dwyer, they have a plan to create a corp procurement policy in Q1 (by March 30) and also look at requirements. Plan to start engaging with vendors early April. 06/01 - Informal meeting to discuss process tentatively booked for 14th January 19th Nov 2024: To help prepare a business case outlining potential solutions and guidance for GrainCorp. Luke to send a detailed follow-up pack, including: Video testimonials, Relevant use cases Zycus offered to conduct discovery workshops to assist GrainCorp in refining their procurement requirements and objectives.
opp.ais_why:
• Across 2,063 chars of NextStep covering 18+ dated entries from Nov 2024 to Apr 2026 ‚Äî including spend analysis project priority, RFP preparation, CPO Kathy Kaderavek's team rebuilds ‚Äî zero mention of AI, Merlin, agentic or GenAI. Product scope contains no AI SKUs (iContract; iSource; iSupplier; iRequest; iRisk). Per the 13 Apr 2026 note: 'Kathy advises project is getting back on track, expects to provide requirements / RFx / sometime in June.'
• On 27 Oct: 'Kathy (CPO) says S2C is on her radar but needs to solve people and process issues first, may be delayed until Q1 2026.' On 4 Mar 2026: 'Project on hold due to team rebuild and BAU requirements, will try again in 3 months.' On 3 Feb 2025: 'GrainCorp have a priority for Spend Analysis, wants a solution in 6-8 weeks. Currently doing discovery on requirements.' Buyer priorities are people/process issues and spend analytics ‚Äî no AI dimension.
• Confidence is Medium given the long timespan (~18 months) and consistent framing. The deal has been repeatedly delayed by internal team rebuilds and BAU pressures. With no AI products in scope and zero AI keyword across 18+ entries, score 2 (AI Resistant) is the clear placement.

items.quote: No buyer-stated financial justification, no metrics tied to S2C initiative; deal lacks commercial urgency
RFP promised June 2026 (per 13 April note) not delivered; no requirements document, no buyer-articulated evaluation criteria
NDA requested by GrainCorp Feb 2025 but no record of NDA being sent or executed; legal workflow stalled or abandoned
'IP believe they may be some help via Infosys' but role unclear; no confirmed partner alignment or co-sell motion

metrics.narrative: No quantified business case or ROI documented across Salesforce fields or recorded buyer conversations. Next Step references (19 Nov 2024) mention 'help prepare a business case outlining potential solutions' but no outcome or buyer-stated financial justification is captured. Early Next Step (3 Feb 2025) notes 'GrainCorp have a priority for Spend Analysis, wants a solution in 6-8 weeks' suggesting urgency on a tactical project, but no metrics tied to the broader S2C initiative (iContract, iSource, iSupplier, iRequest, iRisk per Products__c). Without quantified pain (cost savings, cycle time reduction, error rate, headcount impact) this $300K investment lacks a commercial anchor and will struggle to secure CFO/EB budget approval. The move: run a structured value-discovery session with the CPO and CFO to quantify the S2C business case and tie it to GrainCorp's FY goals.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole (Kathy Kaderavek Decision Maker, Manh Tran Influencer, Astha Muni no role, Ian Morrison no role)
SF Next_Step__c (19-month history, all engagement via CPO)
champion.narrative: No champion is identified. Kathy Kaderavek (CPO, General Manager Procurement) is the Decision Maker per Contact Role and the sole executive contact across 19 months of Next Step history, but she has shown no advocacy for Zycus, no effort to accelerate the process, and no evidence of pushing back on internal delays or competitors on Zycus's behalf. Instead, she has repeatedly deferred engagement (Oct 2025, March 2026, June 2026) citing internal priorities (team rebuilds, people/process issues, BAU overload), signaling her focus is inward (org redesign) rather than outward (vendor selection). She promised an RFP in June 2026 (per 13 April Next Step) but failed to deliver, and has been silent for 47 days. This is passive sponsorship at best, not championship. Manh Tran (Procurement & Contracts Manager, Influencer role) and Astha Muni (Procurement Excellence Analyst) are lower-level contacts with no recorded engagement or advocacy. No multi-threading exists: all engagement flows through the CPO, creating single-point-of-failure risk. Without a champion who has access to the EB/CFO, can drive internal urgency, and will advocate for Zycus in competitive or budget discussions, this deal is organizationally adrift. The move: identify and cultivate a champion within GrainCorp's procurement or finance team who can sponsor Zycus internally, or escalate to Ian Morrison (CFO) to secure executive-level sponsorship and bypass the CPO's chronic delays.

sources: SF Next_Step__c (no competitor mentions except ambiguous Infosys reference 10 March 2025)
competition.narrative: Competitive landscape is entirely unknown. SF Competitors__c is blank, no Next Step entry references a named competitor or competitive benchmark, and no Avoma calls exist to capture buyer statements like 'we are also looking at X' or 'our shortlist includes Y'. The 10 March 2025 Next Step mentions 'IP believe they may be some help via Infosys' but Infosys's role is ambiguous (implementation partner vs competitive displacer vs advisor) and there is no follow-up to clarify whether Infosys is driving vendor selection, bringing an alternative S2C platform, or merely providing services alongside Zycus. GrainCorp may be benchmarking Coupa, SAP Ariba, Ivalua, Jaggaer, or relying on an incumbent ERP procurement module (Oracle, SAP), but Zycus has zero visibility. This blindness is dangerous: a competitor could be advancing in parallel (especially during the 47 days of silence), the buyer could have already selected another vendor and is slow-walking Zycus for leverage, or the RFP (if it ever materializes) could be wired for a preferred vendor. Without competitive intel, Zycus cannot position differentiation, build a wedge strategy, or assess win probability. The move: re-engage the CPO and directly ask who else is being evaluated, what the shortlist looks like, and what GrainCorp's current procurement stack is, framing it as necessary to tailor the Zycus proposal; if the CPO deflects, probe Manh Tran or Astha Muni (procurement team) for competitive context.

identify_pain.narrative: Operational pain is inferred from the Products in scope (iContract, iSource, iSupplier, iRequest, iRisk per SF Products__c) which imply GrainCorp lacks an integrated S2C platform and is likely managing procurement via disconnected systems, spreadsheets, or an inadequate ERP procurement module. A 3 Feb 2025 Next Step notes 'GrainCorp have a priority for Spend Analysis, wants a solution in 6-8 weeks. currently doing discovery on requirements', suggesting pain around spend visibility and analytics, but this appears to be a separate tactical project rather than the S2C suite pain point. The repeated delays and CPO focus on 'people and process issues' (27 Oct Next Step), 'team rebuild' (multiple entries), and 'BAU requirements' (4 March Next Step) suggest the pain is organizationally internal (understaffed procurement, process immaturity, org instability) rather than a burning platform tied to supplier risk, contract leakage, or procurement cycle time. No buyer has articulated the pain on a call (no Avoma transcripts exist), and no Salesforce field captures a confirmed pain statement or pain owner beyond the CPO. Without a confirmed, buyer-owned pain narrative tied to quantified impact, this deal lacks urgency and is vulnerable to indefinite delay. The move: run a pain-validation session with the CPO and a cross-functional team (Finance, Operations) to confirm the business problem, quantify the impact, and identify the pain owner who will champion the S2C initiative internally.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics and legal/procurement workflow are almost entirely unknown. An NDA was requested by GrainCorp in February 2025 (Next Step 13 Feb: 'They advised would need an NDA to progress and would send asap. Still waiting, over a week and been following up multiple contacts regularly'), but there is no record of the NDA being sent by the buyer or executed by either party, and no follow-up is documented after February 2025. This stalled legal step may signal buyer disengagement or internal process breakdown. No information exists on MSA/SOW requirements, legal review cycle, InfoSec approval, procurement channel (direct vs through a partner/SI), or implementation scoping. A 10 March 2025 Next Step mentions 'IP believe they may be some help via Infosys' suggesting potential SI involvement, but Infosys's role (implementation partner vs competitive displacer vs advisor to GrainCorp) is unconfirmed and Zycus has no documented partner alignment or co-sell motion with them. Without clarity on the paper process, Zycus cannot forecast contracting timelines or identify legal/procurement blockers that could delay close even if the commercial deal advances. The move: re-open the NDA conversation to test buyer engagement and use it as a forcing function to surface the broader legal/procurement workflow and any SI/partner dependencies.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole (Ian Morrison, CFO; Kathy Kaderavek, Decision Maker)
SF Next_Step__c (19-month history, no CFO/EB mentions)
economic_buyer.narrative: CFO Ian Morrison is listed in Opportunity Contact Roles but has no recorded engagement in budget approval, pricing discussions, or executive sponsorship of the S2C initiative. Next Step history spanning 19 months contains zero references to CFO involvement or commercial/pricing conversations with any C-level executive. The Decision Maker role is assigned to Kathy Kaderavek (CPO, General Manager Procurement), who has been the sole executive contact, but her authority appears operational (vendor selection, RFP process) rather than budgetary. With no EB engaged and no documented budget authority for a $300K spend, this deal is organizationally orphaned and vulnerable to deprioritization or budget cuts. The absence is particularly concerning given the CPO's repeated internal focus (team rebuilds, BAU overload) rather than outward commercial progression. The move: secure a direct engagement with Ian Morrison (CFO) to confirm budget allocation, executive sponsorship, and commercial timeline before investing further in RFP pursuit.

decision_process.narrative: The decision process is partially reconstructed from Next Step chronology but lacks formal validation or buyer-confirmed milestones. The visible sequence: initial discovery (Nov 2024) → procurement policy + requirements definition (Q1 2025, targeting March 30) → RFP preparation (March 2025) → vendor engagement expected early April 2025 → repeated delays due to CPO team rebuilds and BAU overload (Oct 2025, March 2026, June 2026) → RFP/requirements promised June 2026 (per April update) but not delivered → current silence (47 days, last touch 20 May 2026). The approval chain is unknown: Next Step (13 Feb 2025) mentions 'Graincorp PM is gaining required project approvals prior sending NDA' but no record of NDA execution or who must approve. No clarity on whether CFO Ian Morrison, a procurement committee, or a board must sign off on $300K, or whether the CPO has delegated authority. The lack of NDA follow-through and the chronic reschedules suggest the internal process is either broken or the project is deprioritized. The move: re-engage the CPO to confirm the current state of internal approvals, the actual decision timeline, and whether the S2C initiative is still funded and on the FY roadmap.

sources: SF Next_Step__c (15 Jan 2025, 10 March 2025, 3 Feb 2025, 13 April 2026)
decision_criteria.narrative: GrainCorp's evaluation criteria are only partially visible. Products in scope are confirmed via the relevant stakeholder__c: iContract, iSource, iSupplier, iRequest, iRisk (a full S2C suite). Next Step (15 Jan 2025) notes 'they have a plan to create a corp procurement policy in Q1 (by March 30) and also look at requirements', suggesting criteria definition was underway in early 2025, and 10 March 2025 notes 'In RFP preparation phase' implying requirements were being formalized. However, no RFP or requirements document was ever delivered to Zycus despite multiple commitments (June 2026 per April update), and no Avoma calls exist to capture buyer-articulated evaluation priorities, scoring weights, or must-have vs nice-to-have features. A 3 Feb 2025 Next Step mentions 'GrainCorp have a priority for Spend Analysis, wants a solution in 6-8 weeks' as a potential interim project, but its relationship to the S2C RFP is unclear. Without the RFP or a discovery/workshop session, Zycus is blind to GrainCorp's decision criteria and cannot tailor positioning or identify gaps. The move: demand the long-promised requirements document or RFP, or if stalled, propose a structured discovery workshop to co-create the evaluation framework and lock Zycus into the criteria design.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AI fit signal is unknown. AIS fields (AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c) are not populated, and no Avoma calls exist to capture buyer statements about AI appetite, analytics needs, or Merlin interest. The 3 Feb 2025 Next Step mentions 'priority for Spend Analysis' which could signal appetite for AI-driven spend visibility and insights, but this is an inferred signal, not a confirmed buyer statement. Without direct evidence, AI fit tier cannot be determined.

deal_movement.summary: Deal has been in slow motion for 19 months with repeated buyer-driven delays and broken commitments. Created 19 Nov 2024, advanced to Shortlisted stage at unknown date, and has cycled through multiple CPO-led reschedules (Oct 2025, March 2026, June 2026) driven by internal team rebuilds and BAU overload. Last buyer touch 20 May 2026, now 47 days silent. CPO promised RFP delivery in June 2026 (per 13 April update) but failed to deliver. No Avoma calls exist, so all movement is inferred from Next Step chronology and Salesforce activity timestamps. Deal is cooling with no active buyer engagement, no champion, no EB involvement, and no visibility into whether GrainCorp's S2C initiative is still funded or on the roadmap.

She has delivered no RFP, no NDA follow-through, and has been silent for 47 days. No multi-threading to other stakeholders who could champion internally. Deal is at high risk of indefinite delay or abandonment without a true champion who can drive internal urgency and EB engagement.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 19 Nov 2024, now 595 days old. In Shortlisted stage since unknown date (no stage-date submission fields populated). Last buyer touch 20 May 2026, now 47 days silent. Close date 27 Nov 2026 (144 days out). Next Step history shows pattern of buyer-driven delays across 19 months: NDA promised Feb 2025 never delivered, RFP promised June 2026 (per 13 April note) not delivered, CPO cited team rebuilds and BAU overload in Oct 2025, March 2025, June 2025, and March 2026. No forward slip recorded in field history but chronic buyer-side reschedules push effective timeline right. At 47 days silent with 144 to close, deal requires immediate re-engagement to hit Nov close; current pace (no movement since May) is not defensible for a Shortlisted stage deal 5 months from close.

Currently doing discovery on requirements.' Buyer priorities are people/process issues and spend analytics ‚Äî no AI dimension.
• Confidence is Medium given the long timespan (~18 months) and consistent framing. The deal has been repeatedly delayed by internal team rebuilds and BAU pressures. With no AI products in scope and zero AI keyword across 18+ entries, score 2 (AI Resistant) is the clear placement.
hard.next_step: 25%13 APR - Kathy advises project is getting back on track, expects to provide requirements / RFx / sometime in June.04 March - Project on hold due to team rebuild and BAU requirements, will try again in 3 months27 Oct - Kathy (CPO) says S2C is on her radar but needs to solve people and process issues first, may be delayed until Q1 20262nd June - Progress may be delayed, CPO says she is undertaking another team rebuild, try again in 4 weeks.28/04 - CPO expects to be ready to engage late May or June28/03 - Spoke to CPO, Kathy Kaderavek, still refining requirements and also making procurement team changes. Hopes to be in position to start talking before end of April. Unless serious progress in April, will move this to next quarter10/03 - In RFP preparation phase. IP believe they may be some help via Infosys06/03 - have reached our again to both Astha and Lara Dwyer03/03/25 - RFP for S2P in development. Still waiting to hear if interim Spend Analytics project will proceed20/02/25 - Still waiting on their internal approval to proceed with Spend Analysis project13/02/25 - Graincorp PM is gaining required project approvals prior sending NDA, expects to be in contact before end of month,They advised would need an NDA to progress and would send asap. Still waiting, over a week and been following up multiple contacts regularly.03/02 - GrainCorp have a priority for Spend Analysis, wants a solution in 6-8 weeks. currently doing discovery on requirements.15/01 - Spoke with Lara Dwyer, they have a plan to create a corp procurement policy in Q1 (by March 30) and also look at requirements. Plan to start engaging with vendors early April.06/01 - Informal meeting to discuss process tentatively booked for 14th January19th Nov 2024: To help prepare a business case outlining potential solutions and guidance for GrainCorp.Luke to send a detailed follow-up pack, including: Video testimonials, Relevant use casesZycus offered to conduct discovery workshops to assist GrainCorp in refining their procurement requirements and objectives.

25%

13 APR - Kathy advises project is getting back on track, expects to provide requirements / RFx / sometime in June.

04 March - Project on hold due to team rebuild and BAU requirements, will try again in 3 months

27 Oct - Kathy (CPO) says S2C is on her radar but needs to solve people and process issues first, may be delayed until Q1 2026

2nd June - Progress may be delayed, CPO says she is undertaking another team rebuild, try again in 4 weeks.

28/04 - CPO expects to be ready to engage late May or June

28/03 - Spoke to CPO, Kathy Kaderavek, still refining requirements and also making procurement team changes. Hopes to be in position to start talking before end of April. Unless serious progress in April, will move this to next quarter

10/03 - In RFP preparation phase. IP believe they may be some help via Infosys

06/03 - have reached our again to both Astha and Lara Dwyer

03/03/25 - RFP for S2P in development. Still waiting to hear if interim Spend Analytics project will proceed

20/02/25 - Still waiting on their internal approval to proceed with Spend Analysis project

13/02/25 - Graincorp PM is gaining required project approvals prior sending NDA, expects to be in contact before end of month,

They advised would need an NDA to progress and would send asap. Still waiting, over a week and been following up multiple contacts regularly.

03/02 - GrainCorp have a priority for Spend Analysis, wants a solution in 6-8 weeks. currently doing discovery on requirements.

15/01 - Spoke with Lara Dwyer, they have a plan to create a corp procurement policy in Q1 (by March 30) and also look at requirements. Plan to start engaging with vendors early April.

06/01 - Informal meeting to discuss process tentatively booked for 14th January

19th Nov 2024: To help prepare a business case outlining potential solutions and guidance for GrainCorp.

Luke to send a detailed follow-up pack, including: Video testimonials, Relevant use cases

Zycus offered to conduct discovery workshops to assist GrainCorp in refining their procurement requirements and objectives.
